qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Hardcoded FTP credentials in Charging Gateway Function allow unauthenticated filesystem access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets CGF FTP hardcoded credentials and FTP filesystem access, not the RechargePut out-of-bounds slice access described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing HTTP server timeouts enable Slowloris-style denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets missing http.Server timeout fields and Slowloris-style connection exhaustion, not the RechargePut slice indexing panic.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal via subscriberIdentifier in CDR file writing allows arbitrary file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets unsanitized subscriberIdentifier path construction in CDR file writing, not RechargePut parsing or slice bounds.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via attacker-controlled NotifyUri in converged charging notification
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Uses the recharge endpoint as an SSRF trigger, but the root cause is unvalidated NotifyUri use rather than the advisory's out-of-bounds slice access in RechargePut.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory root cause: an authenticated PUT to the CHF nchf-convergedcharging recharging endpoint reaching github.com/free5gc/chf/internal/sbi.(*Server).RechargePut and triggering an out-of-bounds slice access panic. The qualifying findings describe separate root causes in CGF FTP credentials, HTTP server timeout configuration, CDR path construction, and NotifyUri SSRF.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-6g43-577r-wf4x describes CWE-129 out-of-bounds slice access in github.com/free5gc/chf/internal/sbi.(*Server).RechargePut for PUT /nchf-convergedcharging/v3/recharging/:ueId?ratingGroup=...
ssrf-via-notify-uri reports storage and later use of attacker-controlled notifyUri in SendChargingNotification; it does not report a missing slice bounds check or panic in RechargePut.
path-traversal-via-supi-cdr reports unsanitized subscriberIdentifier concatenation into /tmp/<ueid>.cdr.
hardcoded-ftp-credentials reports static admin/free5gc credentials in internal/cgf/cgf.go.
missing-http-server-timeouts reports absent ReadHeaderTimeout/ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout/IdleTimeout on the CHF HTTP server.
